pharma-life-sciences.cold:
# COLD expansion for Pharma & Life Sciences
blockers_full:
  - id: C1
    eu_ref: "EU GMP Annex 1 (2022); EMA Guidelines Vol. 4"
    impact_full: "All manufacturing suppliers must hold valid EMA/WHO GMP certification; non-certified suppliers are disqualified."
  - id: C2
    eu_ref: "EU GDP Guidelines 2013/C 343/01; EMA/CHMP/QWP guidelines"
    impact_full: "Logistics contracts must specify validated temperature-controlled transport; failure voids batch acceptance and triggers regulatory recall risk."
  - id: C3
    eu_ref: "EU Falsified Medicines Directive 2011/62/EU; Delegated Reg. 2016/161"
    impact_full: "Suppliers must provide serialised packaging with 2D DataMatrix; non-compliant suppliers cannot be onboarded."
high_constraints_full:
  - id: C4
    label: "Patent Expiry Windows (SPC)"
    eu_ref: "EU Supplementary Protection Certificate Reg. 469/2009; SPC Manufacturing Waiver 2019/933"
    impact: "Procurement timing around patent cliffs creates generic substitution opportunities; sourcing strategy must model exclusivity end dates."
  - id: C5
    label: "Batch Traceability Requirements"
    eu_ref: "EU GMP Annex 11; EudraGMDP database requirements"
    impact: "Suppliers must provide full batch documentation; drives supplier evaluation criteria toward ERP/MES integration capability."
  - id: C6
    label: "REACH Chemical Compliance"
    eu_ref: "REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; ECHA SVHC Candidate List"
    impact: "All chemical inputs must be verified on ECHA SVHC list; non-compliant materials trigger reformulation costs that inflate TCO."
  - id: C7
    label: "Pharmacovigilance Reporting Obligations"
    eu_ref: "EU Pharmacovigilance Legislation Dir. 2010/84/EU; EMA/873138/2011"
    impact: "Suppliers of active ingredients may carry reporting obligations; factor compliance infrastructure cost into supplier total cost evaluation."
moderate_constraints:
  - {id: C8, label: "CAPA Completion Requirements", impact: "Open CAPAs against a supplier signal quality instability and are a supplier disqualification risk factor in renewal decisions."}
  - {id: C9, label: "Clinical Trial Ethics Requirements", impact: "Research material procurement must align with ethics approval scope; scope deviations create procurement delays and audit exposure."}
  - {id: C10, label: "Biohazard & Biosafety Protocols", impact: "Suppliers handling BSL-2+ materials must hold certified containment infrastructure; adds mandatory site audit cost to supplier qualification."}
kpis_extended:
  - {id: K5, label: "Inventory Turnover (Expiry Risk)", dir: TARGET_RANGE, signal: "Too low: expiry write-off risk.", lever: "Working Capital Optimisation"}
  - {id: K6, label: "On-Time In-Full Delivery (OTIF)", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "OTIF below 92% in pharma supply chains → production line risk and regulatory batch timing failures.", lever: "Supplier SLA Management"}
  - {id: K7, label: "Audit Pass Rate (First-Time)", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "First-time audit pass rate below 85% = systemic compliance gaps that inflate total procurement risk.", lever: "Audit Readiness"}
  - {id: K8, label: "Cost-per-Unit vs. Medical Inflation Index", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Cost growth exceeding EU HICP Medical sub-index = procurement is failing to offset market inflation.", lever: "Market Benchmarking & Savings Identification"}
  - {id: K9, label: "R&D Procurement Cycle Time", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Cycle time above 90 days for standard research consumables = approval process bottleneck.", lever: "Cycle Time Reduction"}
  - {id: K10, label: "Procurement Savings vs. Reference Price", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Zero or negative savings against published reference prices = weak negotiation leverage or single-source dependency.", lever: "Savings Identification"}

aerospace-defense.cold:
# COLD expansion for Aerospace & Defense
blockers_full:
  - id: C1
    eu_ref: "AS9100 Rev D (IAQG); EASA Part 21 / Part 145 Approvals"
    impact_full: "All production and MRO suppliers must hold AS9100 certification and relevant EASA approvals; non-certified suppliers are disqualified for safety-critical parts."
  - id: C2
    eu_ref: "EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821; EU Arms Export Control framework"
    impact_full: "Procurement of dual-use items and technology requires EU export licence verification; non-compliant supplier jurisdictions trigger disqualification or licence pre-approval."
  - id: C3
    eu_ref: "AS5553 / AS6174 (IAQG standards); EASA CM-21.A-OS-001"
    impact_full: "All electronic and mechanical parts must be sourced through authorised distribution channels; grey market sourcing is a contract termination trigger."
high_constraints_full:
  - id: C4
    label: "NADCAP Special Process Accreditation"
    eu_ref: "NADCAP (international); EASA accepted equivalent processes"
    impact: "Suppliers performing special processes (heat treatment, NDT, welding) must hold valid NADCAP accreditation; expired accreditation halts procurement approval."
  - id: C5
    label: "Cybersecurity Maturity (NIS2 / EUCC)"
    eu_ref: "NIS2 Directive 2022/2555; EU Cybersecurity Certification Scheme (EUCC)"
    impact: "IT and OT system suppliers supporting defence programmes must demonstrate NIS2 compliance; supply chain cyber risk is contractually mandatory to assess."
  - id: C6
    label: "Conflict Minerals Compliance"
    eu_ref: "EU Conflict Minerals Regulation 2017/821 (3TG: tin, tantalum, tungsten, gold)"
    impact: "Suppliers must provide OECD-aligned supply chain due diligence reports; non-reporting suppliers cannot be used in regulated product lines."
  - id: C7
    label: "Material Fatigue Life Documentation"
    eu_ref: "EASA CS-25 / EASA CS-23; EN 9102 First Article Inspection"
    impact: "Procurement must include documented material certifications and traceability records; missing documentation triggers NCR and lot rejection."
moderate_constraints:
  - {id: C8, label: "Long-Term Parts Availability (30+ years)", impact: "Obsolescence risk must be assessed at supplier qualification; single-source components require dual-source strategy or lifetime-buy analysis."}
  - {id: C9, label: "Offset & Industrial Participation Obligations", impact: "Government contracts may require local industrial participation; procurement strategy must model offset credit requirements into supplier selection."}
  - {id: C10, label: "Security Clearance Requirements", impact: "Suppliers accessing classified programme information must hold valid facility security clearances; clearance lead times must be factored into onboarding timelines."}
kpis_extended:
  - {id: K5, label: "Total Cost of Ownership (TCO)", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "TCO analysis omitting obsolescence, logistics, and qualification cost understates true supplier cost by 15-25% in aerospace categories.", lever: "TCO Analysis"}
  - {id: K6, label: "Obsolescence Risk Score", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Components with >30% obsolescence probability within 5 years require immediate DMSMS action or lifetime-buy decision.", lever: "Supply Resilience & Dual-Sourcing"}
  - {id: K7, label: "Lead Time Variance (vs. Contract)", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Variance above 10% consistently = supplier capacity constraint or demand forecasting failure.", lever: "SLA & Delivery Monitoring"}
  - {id: K8, label: "Procurement ROI", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "ROI below 4:1 on procurement investment in aerospace = category management underperformance vs.", lever: "Savings Identification"}
  - {id: K9, label: "Non-Conformance Cost (NCC)", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "NCC above 1.5% of category spend = systemic quality failure; → supplier development programme or exit.", lever: "Quality Cost Tracking"}
  - {id: K10, label: "First-Pass Yield (FPY)", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "FPY below 95% for precision components = process instability; directly inflates unit cost and rework spend.", lever: "TCO & Cost-of-Quality Analysis"}

oil-gas-upstream.cold:
# COLD expansion for Oil & Gas (Upstream)
blockers_full:
  - id: C1
    eu_ref: "EU Offshore Safety Directive 2013/30/EU; SEVESO III Dir. 2012/18/EU"
    impact_full: "All suppliers operating on-site must hold valid HSE qualification; non-compliant suppliers are excluded from site access."
  - id: C2
    eu_ref: "EU Pressure Equipment Directive 2014/68/EU (PED); ATEX Directive 2014/34/EU"
    impact_full: "All pressure vessels and safety-critical equipment must carry PED/CE marking and ATEX certification; non-certified equipment cannot be installed or procured."
  - id: C3
    eu_ref: "EU Offshore Safety Directive 2013/30/EU; OPITO competency standards"
    impact_full: "Offshore suppliers must provide OPITO/BOSIET certified personnel and equipment meeting EU offshore safety standards; non-compliance is a contract termination trigger."
high_constraints_full:
  - id: C4
    label: "Local Content (LC) Quotas"
    eu_ref: "Member State LC regulations (varies by jurisdiction e.g. Norway Act, UK NSTA guidance)"
    impact: "Certain jurisdictions mandate minimum local content percentages; procurement strategy must model LC compliance targets into supplier selection."
  - id: C5
    label: "Pipeline Integrity Regulations"
    eu_ref: "EU Industrial Emissions Directive 2010/75/EU; EN 1594 / ISO 13623"
    impact: "Pipeline inspection and maintenance suppliers must demonstrate competency against relevant EN/ISO standards; integrity failures trigger regulatory shutdown."
  - id: C6
    label: "Decommissioning Liability Management"
    eu_ref: "OSPAR Convention; EU Environmental Liability Directive 2004/35/CE"
    impact: "Decommissioning cost obligations must be modelled into long-term supplier contracts; procurement must avoid supplier lock-in that inflates decommissioning TCO."
  - id: C7
    label: "Spill Response & Environmental Permits"
    eu_ref: "EU Water Framework Directive 2000/60/EC; MARPOL (via EU Maritime Law)"
    impact: "Suppliers providing containment and emergency response services must hold pre-approved permits; procurement lead times must account for regulatory pre-approval."
moderate_constraints:
  - {id: C8, label: "Weather-Dependent Logistics Constraints", impact: "Offshore logistics contracts must specify weather-window SLAs; procurement must model seasonal logistics cost into TCO for remote operations."}
  - {id: C9, label: "Remote Area Operations Requirements", impact: "Remote operations require suppliers with proven logistics and MEDEVAC capability; total cost must include mobilisation, accommodation, and rotation costs."}
  - {id: C10, label: "Emissions Reporting (EU ETS)", impact: "Offshore operations within EU ETS scope must track and report Scope 3 supplier emissions; procurement must request supplier carbon footprint data."}
kpis_extended:
  - {id: K5, label: "Spend Under Management (SUM)", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "SUM below 70% in upstream operations = high maverick spend exposure, particularly in emergency maintenance categories.", lever: "Spend Analytics & Category Management"}
  - {id: K6, label: "Asset Utilisation Rate", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Asset utilisation below 75% = overcapacity in equipment fleet; → utilisation-based contract restructuring.", lever: "Asset TCO Optimisation"}
  - {id: K7, label: "Logistics Cost per Barrel (Offshore)", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Logistics cost per barrel above regional benchmark by >20% = inefficient supply base or unoptimised freight routing.", lever: "Logistics Cost Modelling"}
  - {id: K8, label: "Savings vs. Brent Volatility Exposure", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Inability to demonstrate procurement savings vs.", lever: "Market Intelligence & Price Signals"}
  - {id: K9, label: "Supplier Reliability Score", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Reliability score below 85% for critical equipment suppliers is a contract renegotiation trigger.", lever: "Supplier Performance Evaluation"}
  - {id: K10, label: "Emergency Purchase Ratio", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Emergency purchase ratio above 15% = inventory management failure; emergency purchases carry 30-50% price premium vs.", lever: "Supply Resilience & Inventory Optimisation"}

automotive-oem.cold:
# COLD expansion for Automotive (OEM)
blockers_full:
  - id: C1
    eu_ref: "IATF 16949:2016 (IATG/ISO); EU Type Approval Reg. 2018/858"
    impact_full: "All production part suppliers must hold valid IATF 16949 certification; non-certified suppliers are disqualified from series production sourcing."
  - id: C2
    eu_ref: "EU Industrial Policy framework; OEM-specific supply contracts"
    impact_full: "Suppliers must demonstrate proven JIT/JIS delivery capability with buffer stock protocols; inability to meet takt-time delivery is a disqualifier for Tier-1 sourcing."
  - id: C3
    eu_ref: "EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542; ECE-R100 (UNECE via EU)"
    impact_full: "Battery system suppliers must comply with EU Battery Regulation including carbon footprint declaration and supply chain due diligence from 2025; non-compliance blocks market access."
high_constraints_full:
  - id: C4
    label: "PPAP Approval Cycles"
    eu_ref: "AIAG PPAP 4th Ed. (industry standard); VDA 2 (German OEM equivalent)"
    impact: "New parts require full PPAP/VDA 2 approval before series production; PPAP lead time (12-20 weeks) must be built into supplier onboarding timelines."
  - id: C5
    label: "Raw Material Price Indexation"
    eu_ref: "EU CBAM (Carbon Border Adjustment) Reg. 2023/956; LME pricing references"
    impact: "Steel, aluminium, copper, and battery mineral contracts must include price indexation clauses; fixed-price contracts expose OEM to commodity volatility."
  - id: C6
    label: "Recyclability & End-of-Life Targets"
    eu_ref: "EU End-of-Life Vehicles Directive (ELV) revision (2023 proposal); EU Battery Regulation recycled content targets"
    impact: "Materials procurement must track recyclability rates; non-compliant materials create ELV compliance exposure and restrict future vehicle type approval."
  - id: C7
    label: "Tooling Ownership & IP Laws"
    eu_ref: "EU IP framework; OEM tooling ownership agreements (jurisdiction-specific)"
    impact: "Tooling contracts must clearly define OEM ownership rights; ambiguous tooling IP creates supplier lock-in and inflates switching cost."
moderate_constraints:
  - {id: C8, label: "Warranty Liability & Field Quality Obligations", impact: "Supplier warranty obligations must be contractually aligned with OEM customer warranty terms; misalignment creates uncapped liability exposure."}
  - {id: C9, label: "Tiered Supply Chain Transparency", impact: "Under CSDDD, OEMs must map Tier-2+ supply chain for human rights and environmental risk; procurement must capture sub-supplier data for compliance reporting."}
  - {id: C10, label: "EU Supply Chain Due Diligence (ESG)", impact: "Supplier ESG scorecards must be integrated into sourcing decisions; inability to report on Scope 3 supplier emissions creates CSRD reporting gaps."}
kpis_extended:
  - {id: K5, label: "Supply Lead Time (vs. Target)", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Lead time variance above 15% vs.", lever: "Procurement Cycle Acceleration"}
  - {id: K6, label: "Maverick Spend %", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Maverick spend above 10% in a controlled OEM category = procurement process bypass; creates quality and liability risk.", lever: "Spend Analytics & Contract Compliance"}
  - {id: K7, label: "Order Fill Rate", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Fill rate below 97% creates buffer stock depletion risk; in JIT environments this directly threatens production continuity.", lever: "Inventory & Demand Management"}
  - {id: K8, label: "Production Downtime Rate (Supplier-attributed)", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Any supplier-attributed downtime event → root cause analysis; repeat events are a contractual exit trigger.", lever: "Downtime Cost & Supplier Impact Analysis"}
  - {id: K9, label: "Purchase Price Variance (PPV) vs. Index", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Unfavourable PPV above 2% vs.", lever: "Market Benchmarking & Savings"}
  - {id: K10, label: "Inventory Value vs. Target", dir: TARGET_RANGE, signal: "Excess inventory = demand forecasting failure; insufficient inventory = JIT buffer policy breach.", lever: "Working Capital Optimisation"}

banking-finance.cold:
# COLD expansion for Banking & Finance
blockers_full:
  - id: C1
    eu_ref: "EU AML Directive 6AMLD 2018/1673/EU; EBA AML/CFT Guidelines"
    impact_full: "All third-party vendors processing financial data must pass KYC onboarding; non-compliant vendors disqualified."
  - id: C2
    eu_ref: "GDPR (EU) 2016/679; EBA Outsourcing Guidelines EBA/GL/2019/02"
    impact_full: "Cloud and SaaS vendors must offer EU data residency; US-only hosting is a disqualifier without valid SCCs and supplementary measures."
  - id: C3
    eu_ref: "Digital Operational Resilience Act (EU) 2022/2554 — effective Jan 2025"
    impact_full: "ICT vendors classified as critical must register with ESA; procurement must include contractual resilience testing rights and exit strategy documentation."
high_constraints_full:
  - id: C4
    label: "Outsourcing Risk Concentration Limits"
    eu_ref: "EBA/GL/2019/02 — Outsourcing Arrangements; ECB supervisory expectations"
    impact: "Concentration in a single cloud provider or critical vendor may breach supervisory thresholds; drives mandatory multi-vendor sourcing strategy."
  - id: C5
    label: "Cybersecurity Standards (NIS2)"
    eu_ref: "NIS2 Directive (EU) 2022/2555; ENISA cybersecurity guidelines"
    impact: "Critical infrastructure vendors must demonstrate NIS2 compliance; procurement must include security audit rights and incident notification SLAs."
  - id: C6
    label: "Step-in Rights & Exit Strategy Mandates"
    eu_ref: "ECB Supervisory Expectations; EBA Outsourcing Guidelines §13"
    impact: "Contracts must include documented exit plans and regulator step-in rights; non-negotiable clause that affects vendor selection and contract structure."
moderate_constraints:
  - {id: C7, label: "Regulatory Capital Impact of Vendor Relationships", impact: "Certain vendor arrangements affect capital adequacy calculations; procurement must flag contracts above materiality thresholds to the Risk function."}
  - {id: C8, label: "Audit Access Rights (Regulatory)", impact: "Vendors must contractually permit regulator and internal audit access; refusal is a disqualification criterion."}
  - {id: C9, label: "Business Continuity / Vendor BCP Requirements", impact: "Critical vendors must provide BCP documentation; procurement must verify recovery time objectives align with the institution's own RTO requirements."}
  - {id: C10, label: "ESG & Supplier Diversity Reporting (CSRD)", impact: "Under CSRD, spend with diverse and sustainable suppliers must be reportable; inability to capture supplier ESG data is a compliance reporting gap."}
kpis_extended:
  - {id: K5, label: "AP Processing Time", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Processing time above 10 days = manual bottleneck; increases late payment risk and damages supplier relationship leverage.", lever: "AP & Invoice Automation"}
  - {id: K6, label: "Data Breach Incident Rate (Vendor-attributed)", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Any vendor-attributed breach → mandatory GDPR Article 33 notification within 72 hours; repeat incidents are disqualification triggers.", lever: "Vendor Security Audit"}
  - {id: K7, label: "Procurement ROI vs. IT Budget", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "ROI below 3:1 on procurement investment = category management failure; FS sector benchmark is 4-6:1.", lever: "Savings Identification"}
  - {id: K8, label: "Vendor Diversity Spend %", dir: TARGET_RANGE, signal: "Under CSRD, diversity spend must be measurable and reportable; inability to report is a compliance gap flagged in", lever: "ESG & Sustainable Procurement"}
  - {id: K9, label: "Regulatory Audit Finding Rate (Procurement)", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Any procurement-related Major Finding in a supervisory audit → Board-level reporting obligation and remediation programme.", lever: "Audit Readiness"}
  - {id: K10, label: "Savings vs. Category Benchmark", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Savings below category benchmark = missed negotiation leverage; particularly impactful in IT and professional services spend.", lever: "Market Benchmarking"}

construction-infrastructure.cold:
# COLD expansion for Construction & Infrastructure
blockers_full:
  - id: C1
    eu_ref: "EU Construction Products Regulation 305/2011 (CPR); national building codes"
    impact_full: "All structural materials and systems must carry CE marking under CPR; non-CE materials cannot be specified or procured for EU regulated projects."
  - id: C2
    eu_ref: "EU-OSHA Construction Directive 92/57/EEC; Machinery Regulation 2023/1230"
    impact_full: "All subcontractors must hold valid H&S qualification and site-specific induction; failure to comply triggers site suspension and client penalty clauses."
  - id: C3
    eu_ref: "EU EIA Directive 2011/92/EU; SEA Directive 2001/42/EC"
    impact_full: "Procurement of site services must not commence before environmental impact assessment sign-off; premature mobilisation creates regulatory penalty exposure."
high_constraints_full:
  - id: C4
    label: "Subcontractor Liability & Bonding"
    eu_ref: "EU Late Payment Directive 2011/7/EU; national surety bond frameworks"
    impact: "Subcontractors above value thresholds must provide performance and payment bonds; procurement must verify bonding capacity before award."
  - id: C5
    label: "Labour Law & Posted Workers Compliance"
    eu_ref: "Posted Workers Directive 2018/957/EU; national minimum wage regulations"
    impact: "Cross-border subcontracting must comply with posted workers rules; non-compliant arrangements trigger joint liability for unpaid wages and social contributions."
  - id: C6
    label: "LEED / BREEAM Sustainability Targets"
    eu_ref: "EU Green Deal; EU Taxonomy Regulation 2020/852; Nearly Zero Energy Buildings (NZEB) Dir. 2010/31/EU"
    impact: "Sustainable construction certifications require procurement of certified materials and qualified contractors; spec deviations risk certification downgrade and client penalty."
  - id: C7
    label: "Payment Milestone & Retention Laws"
    eu_ref: "EU Late Payment Directive 2011/7/EU; national construction payment legislation"
    impact: "Retention and milestone payment terms must comply with legal maximums; non-compliant terms create regulatory liability and supplier relationship risk."
moderate_constraints:
  - {id: C8, label: "Force Majeure & Material Price Escalation Clauses", impact: "Contracts must include tested force majeure and material price escalation provisions; absence exposes buyer to uncapped cost variance in volatile markets."}
  - {id: C9, label: "Site Access & Logistics Constraints", impact: "Material delivery schedules must account for urban access restrictions and permit lead times; failure to plan logistics constraints inflates project overhead cost."}
  - {id: C10, label: "Waste Management & Circular Economy Obligations", impact: "Demolition and construction waste must be segregated and disposed of by licensed contractors; non-compliant waste disposal creates regulatory and reputational liability."}
kpis_extended:
  - {id: K5, label: "Lead Time Variance (Materials)", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Material lead time variance above 15% vs.", lever: "Supply Chain & Delivery Monitoring"}
  - {id: K6, label: "Change Order Cost % of Contract", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Change order cost above 10% of contract value = poor initial scope definition; above 20% = scope management", lever: "Scope Change Cost Analysis"}
  - {id: K7, label: "Equipment Utilisation Rate", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Plant utilisation below 65% = over-procurement or poor scheduling; directly inflates day-rate cost per unit of output.", lever: "Asset Utilisation & TCO"}
  - {id: K8, label: "Subcontractor Quality Score", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Quality score below 75% → formal defect remediation programme; persistent failure leads to subcontractor blacklist.", lever: "Subcontractor Performance Evaluation"}
  - {id: K9, label: "Labour Idle Time %", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Idle time above 15% = scheduling or supply chain coordination failure; directly inflates cost-per-unit-of-output.", lever: "Labour Cost & Productivity Analysis"}
  - {id: K10, label: "Savings % on Subcontracting vs. Benchmark", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Savings below zero vs.", lever: "Market Benchmarking & Tender Analysis"}

chemicals.cold:
# COLD expansion for Chemicals
blockers_full:
  - id: C1
    eu_ref: "REACH (EC) 1907/2006; CLP Regulation (EC) 1272/2008"
    impact_full: "All chemical substances must be REACH registered and CLP classified; procurement of unregistered substances for EU market use is prohibited."
  - id: C2
    eu_ref: "SEVESO III Directive 2012/18/EU"
    impact_full: "Suppliers operating SEVESO-classified sites must provide safety reports and emergency plans; procurement must verify site classification before supplier engagement."
  - id: C3
    eu_ref: "EU Industrial Emissions Directive 2010/75/EU; ATEX Directive 2014/34/EU"
    impact_full: "Suppliers storing or handling hazardous substances must hold valid environmental permits; expired or absent permits are disqualification criteria."
high_constraints_full:
  - id: C4
    label: "EU Emissions Caps (EU ETS)"
    eu_ref: "EU ETS Directive 2003/87/EC (revised 2023); CBAM Reg. 2023/956"
    impact: "Energy-intensive chemical suppliers covered by EU ETS must provide verified emissions data; CBAM exposure must be factored into import procurement cost modelling."
  - id: C5
    label: "Waste Disposal Permits & Circular Economy"
    eu_ref: "EU Waste Framework Directive 2008/98/EC; EU Chemicals Strategy for Sustainability"
    impact: "Hazardous waste disposal suppliers must hold valid ADR/waste transfer permits; procurement must verify disposal chain compliance to avoid joint liability."
  - id: C6
    label: "SDS (Safety Data Sheet) Documentation"
    eu_ref: "REACH Article 31; CLP Regulation Annex II; EU SDS format Reg. 2020/878"
    impact: "Suppliers must provide REACH-compliant SDS for all chemical products; absent or non-compliant SDS blocks product receipt and triggers recall risk."
  - id: C7
    label: "Feedstock Price Volatility Management"
    eu_ref: "EU Energy Market Regulation; LME / ICIS index references"
    impact: "Feedstock contracts must include price indexation clauses linked to published benchmarks (ICIS, Argus); fixed-price arrangements expose buyers to uncapped volatility."
moderate_constraints:
  - {id: C8, label: "Biosecurity & Site Security Protocols (CFATS equivalent)", impact: "Suppliers of dual-use chemicals must provide end-use declarations; procurement must verify export control status before order placement."}
  - {id: C9, label: "Tanker & Transport Safety Certifications", impact: "Chemical logistics suppliers must hold valid ADR certifications; transport of hazardous goods by non-certified carriers creates regulatory liability."}
  - {id: C10, label: "CO2 Reduction & Sustainability Targets", impact: "Suppliers must provide carbon footprint data per product/batch for Scope 3 reporting; inability to provide data creates CSRD reporting gaps."}
kpis_extended:
  - {id: K5, label: "Safety Audit Score", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Safety audit score below 80% → mandatory supplier corrective action plan; below 60% is a contract suspension threshold.", lever: "Supplier HSE Audit"}
  - {id: K6, label: "Inventory Accuracy %", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Inventory accuracy below 97% in hazardous chemical storage creates SEVESO compliance risk and potential regulatory reporting breach.", lever: "Inventory Control & Compliance"}
  - {id: K7, label: "Maintenance Downtime (Supplier-attributed)", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Supplier-attributed downtime above 2% of scheduled production hours → contractual SLA review and penalty assessment.", lever: "Downtime Cost Analysis"}
  - {id: K8, label: "CO2 Reduction Tons (vs. Baseline)", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Failure to demonstrate year-on-year CO2 reduction creates CSRD reporting gap and EU ETS compliance exposure.", lever: "ESG & Sustainable Procurement Targets"}
  - {id: K9, label: "Supplier Availability Score", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Availability score below 95% for single-source feedstocks → dual-source qualification requirement.", lever: "Supply Resilience & Dual-Sourcing"}
  - {id: K10, label: "TCO Lifecycle vs. Benchmark", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "TCO above peer group benchmark by >10% = category management underperformance requiring supplier renegotiation or substitution.", lever: "TCO Analysis"}

medical-devices.cold:
# COLD expansion for Medical Devices
blockers_full:
  - id: C1
    eu_ref: "ISO 13485:2016; EU MDR 2017/745 Annex IX"
    impact_full: "All component and subassembly suppliers must hold ISO 13485 certification; non-certified suppliers are disqualified from regulated supply chain."
  - id: C2
    eu_ref: "EU Medical Devices Regulation 2017/745 (MDR); EU IVDR 2017/746"
    impact_full: "All device components must be traceable to EU MDR technical documentation; procurement of non-compliant components creates market access risk."

  - {id: K8, label: "Soil Health Index (vs. Target)", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Soil health index deterioration = input and agronomy procurement strategy creating long-term yield loss; EU Farm to Fork", lever: "ESG & Long-Term Asset Management"}
  - {id: K9, label: "Equipment Uptime % (Seasonal Critical)", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Harvesting and planting equipment downtime during seasonal peak creates irreversible yield loss; maintenance contract performance is the critical", lever: "Equipment Performance & Maintenance"}
  - {id: K10, label: "Water Cost % of Production Cost", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Water cost above benchmark = inefficient irrigation technology or abstraction pricing structure; also creates WFD compliance pressure in", lever: "Water Cost Optimisation & ESG"}

electronics.cold:
# COLD expansion for Electronics
blockers_full:
  - id: C1
    eu_ref: "EU RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU (RoHS3); EU WEEE Directive 2012/19/EU"
    impact_full: "All electronic components must be RoHS-compliant; non-compliant substances are banned from EU market; WEEE take-back obligations must be factored into supplier contracts."
  - id: C2
    eu_ref: "EU LVD Directive 2014/35/EU; EU EMC Directive 2014/30/EU; EU Radio Equipment Dir. 2014/53/EU"
    impact_full: "All electronic products must carry CE marking with supporting technical file; non-CE products cannot be placed on EU market."
  - id: C3
    eu_ref: "EU Conflict Minerals Regulation 2017/821 (3TG); OECD Due Diligence Guidance"
    impact_full: "Tin, tantalum, tungsten, and gold supply chains must include OECD-aligned due diligence reporting; non-reporting suppliers cannot be used in regulated product lines."
high_constraints_full:
  - id: C4
    label: "EU Battery Regulation (EV & Consumer)"
    eu_ref: "EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542"
    impact: "Battery cell and pack suppliers must comply with EU Battery Regulation carbon footprint declarations and supply chain due diligence from 2025; non-compliant batteries cannot be placed on EU market."
  - id: C5
    label: "Chip Sourcing Ethics & Export Controls"
    eu_ref: "EU Chips Act 2023/1781; EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821"
    impact: "Semiconductor procurement must comply with EU Dual-Use Regulation; chips with controlled specifications require export licence; EU Chips Act creates new strategic stockpiling considerations."
  - id: C6
    label: "Cybersecurity & Firmware (EU CRA)"
    eu_ref: "EU Cyber Resilience Act 2024/2847"
    impact: "Networked electronic products sold in EU must meet CRA essential cybersecurity requirements from Dec 2027; procurement must assess vendor CRA readiness timeline in current sourcing decisions."
  - id: C7
    label: "Energy Star / Eco-design Requirements"
    eu_ref: "EU Ecodesign Regulation 2024; EU Energy Labelling Reg. 2017/1369"
    impact: "Electronic product procurement must meet EU Ecodesign efficiency requirements; non-compliant products face market restriction; energy labelling must be verified at procurement stage."
moderate_constraints:
  - {id: C8, label: "Patent Licensing & Freedom to Operate", impact: "Component procurement must be accompanied by freedom-to-operate assessment for key patents; unresolved patent exposure creates injunction risk and product recall liability."}
  - {id: C9, label: "Battery Disposal & Circular Economy", impact: "Battery suppliers must demonstrate compliance with collection rate and recycled content targets; non-compliant suppliers create EU Battery Regulation exposure at import stage."}
  - {id: C10, label: "Labour Audit (Factory Ethics — IP/Human Rights)", impact: "Electronics factory audits must verify compliance with ILO core labour standards; CSDDD requires documented human rights due diligence across the electronics supply chain."}
kpis_extended:
  - {id: K5, label: "Savings on Assembly vs. Benchmark", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Assembly cost above industry benchmark (ECIA, IDC) = contract not competitive; China+1 strategy may offer 15-25% cost reduction", lever: "Manufacturing Cost Benchmarking"}
  - {id: K6, label: "Inventory Turnover (Component Stock)", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Turnover below 6x annually = excess inventory with obsolescence risk; semiconductor product cycles create 12-18 month obsolescence windows.", lever: "Working Capital & Obsolescence Risk"}
  - {id: K7, label: "Component Longevity (vs. Product Life)", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Component end-of-life notification before product end-of-life creates lifetime-buy or redesign decision; procurement must track component lifecycle dates.", lever: "Supply Resilience & Lifecycle Management"}
  - {id: K8, label: "OEE (PCB Assembly Lines)", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "PCB assembly OEE below 70% = significant availability or quality loss in contract manufacturing; → EMS provider performance", lever: "Manufacturing Asset Efficiency"}
  - {id: K9, label: "Tooling Amortisation Rate (vs. Volume)", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Tooling amortisation below plan due to volume shortfall = demand forecast failure; creates stranded tooling cost that inflates", lever: "CapEx & Tooling TCO Analysis"}
  - {id: K10, label: "Supplier Concentration Risk (Single-Source %)", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Single-source dependency above 50% for critical semiconductors or displays is the highest supply chain risk in electronics; EU", lever: "Supply Resilience & Dual-Sourcing"}

sea-logistics.cold:
# COLD expansion for Sea Logistics
blockers_full:
  - id: C1
    eu_ref: "EU MRV Maritime Regulation 2015/757; EU ETS extension to shipping 2024 (Dir. 2003/87/EC)"
    impact_full: "Ocean freight carrier contracts must specify CII rating and EU ETS compliance; procurement must model EU ETS shipping surcharges into total sea freight TCO from 2024."
  - id: C2
    eu_ref: "EU Port Security Regulation 725/2004; EU Maritime Security Regulation 725/2004"
    impact_full: "Port agents and terminal operators must hold valid ISPS declarations; non-compliant port agents create cargo clearance delays, customs holds, and security liability."
  - id: C3
    eu_ref: "EU Union Customs Code 952/2013; EU ICS2 (Import Control System 2) from 2024"
    impact_full: "Freight forwarders must submit ICS2-compliant advance cargo declarations; non-compliant declarations trigger customs inspection, cargo detention, and delivery delay."
high_constraints_full:
  - id: C4
    label: "Ballast Water Management"
    eu_ref: "IMO BWM Convention 2004 (EU implementing regulation); EU Marine Strategy Dir. 2008/56/EC"
    impact: "Vessel operators must hold valid BWM certificates; non-compliant vessels face port state control detention; procurement must verify BWM compliance in charter agreements."
  - id: C5
    label: "Container Safety & CSC Certification"
    eu_ref: "CSC Convention (EU implementing regulation); EU Container Regulation 1999"
    impact: "Container leasing and purchase suppliers must provide valid CSC safety approval plates; non-certified containers cannot be used for international cargo transport."
  - id: C6
    label: "Bill of Lading Legal Standards"
    eu_ref: "EU Hamburg Rules implementation; Rotterdam Rules (pending); CMR Convention"
    impact: "Shipping line and NVOCC contracts must specify B/L legal regime; ambiguous B/L terms create cargo claim liability; electronic B/L standards must be contractually confirmed."
  - id: C7
    label: "Anti-Bribery & Maritime Corruption"
    eu_ref: "EU Anti-Corruption Directive (proposed 2023/0135); OECD Maritime anti-bribery guidelines"
    impact: "Port agent and customs broker procurement must include anti-bribery representations; facilitation payments in maritime corridors create direct corporate liability under EU frameworks."
moderate_constraints:
  - {id: C8, label: "Pilotage & Port Entry Requirements", impact: "Port entry procurement must include pilotage costs for compulsory pilotage ports; omission from freight cost modelling inflates landed cost variance."}
  - {id: C9, label: "Cabotage Laws", impact: "Carrier selection for intra-EU coastal transport must comply with EU cabotage regulation; non-compliant carrier use creates regulatory enforcement and cargo seizure risk."}
  - {id: C10, label: "Ship Registry & Flag State Laws", impact: "Vessel charter procurement must verify flag state compliance and ship recycling regulation adherence; sub-standard flag states create port state control risk and reputational exposure."}
kpis_extended:
  - {id: K5, label: "Cargo Damage Rate %", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Damage rate above 0.3% of TEUs = packing, handling, or stowage failure; → cargo insurance claim and carrier", lever: "Cargo Quality & Insurance Claims"}
  - {id: K6, label: "Fuel Surcharge Variance vs. Bunker Index", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "BAF above published Bunker Adjustment Factor formula by >5% = freight invoice audit required; systematic BAF overcharge is", lever: "Freight Cost Optimisation & Market Benchmarking"}
  - {id: K7, label: "Blank Sailing Frequency (Carrier)", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Blank sailing frequency above 15% of scheduled departures = carrier network rationalisation; requires procurement to activate alternative routing", lever: "Market Intelligence & Carrier Capacity Signals"}
  - {id: K8, label: "Emissions per TEU (CO2e)", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "CO2 per TEU above lane benchmark = non-optimised vessel selection or routing; EU ETS cost makes this metric", lever: "ESG & Sustainable Logistics Procurement"}
  - {id: K9, label: "Freight Bill Accuracy (Invoice Audit)", dir: HIGHER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Bill accuracy below 95% = rate card errors or accessorial charge inflation; systematic freight audit typically recovers 2-4%", lever: "Invoice Audit & Freight Cost Control"}
  - {id: K10, label: "Lead Time (Port to Door — Total)", dir: LOWER_IS_BETTER, signal: "Total lead time above contracted transit plus standard inland transport = customs clearance or port drayage failure; creates", lever: "Supply Chain Lead Time Management"}
